content.problemStatement:
Chef has an array AAA of NNN positive integers. Chef can perform the following operation any (possibly zero) number of times on the array.


Choose any index iii such that (1<i≤N)(1 \lt i \le N)(1<i≤N);
Choose an integer XXX such that (1≤X≤Ai)(1\le X \le A_i)(1≤X≤Ai​);
Set A(i−1):=A(i−1)+XA_{(i-1)} := A_{(i-1)} + XA(i−1)​:=A(i−1)​+X;
Set Ai:=Ai−XA_i := A_i - XAi​:=Ai​−X.
Help Chef to **minimize** the **maximum** value of array AAA using the above operations .


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the size of array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-seperated integers, the elements of array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the minimized maximum value possible of array AAA.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 10^6
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated positive integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the minimum possible value of the maximum element of the array after performing any number of allowed operations.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case compute prefix sums and take the maximum of ceil(prefixSum/i). The results are 5, 13 and 10 respectively.
Prefix sums: 10, 30. Ceil averages: 10, 15. Maximum is 15.
Prefix sums: 2,10,13,20,26. Ceil averages: 2,5,5,5,6. Maximum is 6.

solution.approach: The operation can move any amount from position i to i‑1, therefore the total sum of the first i elements can never be reduced. After all moves, the first i positions will contain exactly the sum of the original prefix of length i, but this sum can be distributed arbitrarily among those i positions. The smallest possible maximum among the first i positions is ceil(prefixSum_i / i). To make the whole array as balanced as possible we must satisfy this bound for every i, so the answer is the maximum of ceil(prefixSum_i / i) over i = 1..N. We compute prefix sums while scanning the array once, keep the running maximum of the ceil values, and output it. The algorithm is O(N) per test case and uses O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.buffer.read().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        s = 0
        ans = 0
        for i in range(1, N+1):
            x = int(next(it))
            s += x
            cur = (s + i - 1) // i  # ceil division
            if cur > ans:
                ans = cur
        out_lines.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
